---
title: "Rmarkdown as notebook"
author: "Ruben Arslan"
date: "2019-07-12"
output:
  html_document
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: inline
---

# Introduction

For example, you can make enlarge-able plots:

```{r echo=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(cache = TRUE,autodep=TRUE)
```

## Random number 1
```{r}
library(rmdpartials)

partial(text = "`r runif(1)`")
times = 100
```

## Random number 2
```{r}
make_random_number <- function(times = 10) {
  random_number = runif(1) * times
  partial(text = "`r random_number`")
}
make_random_number <- function(times = 10) {
  random_number = runif(1) * times
  partial(text = paste0("`","``{r}
plot(random_number)
``","`"))
}
make_random_number(times)
```

## Random number 3
```{r}
make_random_number()
```


## Random number 4
```{r}
make_two_random_numbers <- function(times) {
  partial(text = paste0("`","``{r}
make_random_number(times)
``","`","

`","``{r}
make_random_number(times)
``","`"))
}
make_two_random_numbers(times)
```

## Random number 5
```{r}
make_two_random_numbers(times)
```
